key_insight: Random sampling maps provide a unified way to compare inputs of different sizes and to approximate large structured inputs by smaller sketches, enabling size-generalization analysis.
engineering_relevance: This gives principled guidance for building scalable models on sequences, point clouds, graphs, and tensors when training and inference sizes differ.
method: Define sampling notions that generalize sampling with replacement, random binning, and species sampling.
Match each sampling scheme to domain symmetries and size relations to determine when it is appropriate.
Derive explicit generalization and sketching rates for function classes continuous under the chosen sampling notion.
evidence_signal: The paper provides theoretical generalization and sketching rates, with examples spanning measures, graph homomorphism densities, permutation-invariant transformers, and graph neural networks.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on selecting a sampling model aligned with the domain’s symmetries, so a mismatched choice may weaken or invalidate the bounds.
